title: CVE-2025-22869 x/crypto v0.35.0
body:
Bump golang.org/x/crypto to v0.35.0

This will resolve CVE-2025-22869

Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-81326, https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-82777

Ugh, I'm not sure about the Go bump to v1.23.0....
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
Guess we need cirrus image updates with go 1.23. Packit builds pass fwiw.

> Guess we need cirrus image updates with go 1.23. Packit builds pass fwiw.

ah there's f40 job failing too. I guess that'd go away whenever f40 gets 1.23

>  but I see x/crypto latest is v0.36.0. Is there anything blocking switch to that?

Yes we should update to the latest.



> Ugh, I'm not sure about the Go bump to v1.23.0....

Like I said on slack, CI is not ready for go 1.23, we either wait a few weeks and F40 likely gets and go 1.23 rebase or we have to update CI to f41/f42 beta. Last time we talked about this we said we can wait a few weeks so I have no taken any action either way.

Of course if we have to patch this *now* then this changes things. How much time do we have? I rather not rush the go 1.23 update.

> Of course if we have to patch this _now_ then this changes things. How much time do we have? I rather not rush the go 1.23 update.

I'm cool with waiting until things land in F40. It will be EOL soon anyway. FWIW, old-stable Fedora (in this case f40) bodhi errata usually don't get karma that quick. So I suspect most people have moved on to latest-stable already.

> I'm cool with waiting until things land in F40. It will be EOL soon anyway. FWIW, old-stable Fedora (in this case f40) bodhi errata usually don't get karma that quick. So I suspect most people have moved on to latest-stable already.

Above was RE: downstream Fedora. I'm fine with waiting either way.

For everyone's record, F40's EOL I think would be late May. So, we have 2+ months to switch to F41 (preferably F42). And go1.23 on F40 has +4 votes and no negatives, so it's likely to happen: https://pagure.io/fesco/issue/3372

@lsm5 I am sure it happens, but it doesn't mean it happens right away. Someone would still need to propose the update in fedora, then it must pass tests, needs karma, etc... Only once it lands in stable I can update our VM images, then who knows what regressions we find there. Only once all that is done we can update. And then we get to backport the VM image updates to the release branches.

That why I ask how much time we have? Because most of the above must happen anyway. The only choice we have is to just drop all f40 jobs in CI if it is urgent enough.
